summaryAm: ንጉሥ የተፈጥሮ ዘፈን ከብሩህ መጫወቻ በላይ የልብን ደስታ እንደሚሰጥ የሚማርበት ታሪክ።
moralAm: እውነተኛ ውበት በቀላል እና በልብ የመጣ ድምፅ ውስጥ ይገኛል።

source.name: Hans Christian Andersen tale
source.notes: Public-domain source inspiration only. This is an original child-friendly Amharic adaptation with softened themes for ages 3-6.

pages.textAm: በአንድ ቆንጆ አትክልት ውስጥ ትንሽ የሌሊት ወፍ በጣም ደስ የሚል ትዘምር ነበር።
pages.textEn: In a beautiful garden, a small nightingale sang a very lovely song.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Nightingale, page 1: በአንድ ቆንጆ አትክልት ውስጥ ትንሽ የሌሊት ወፍ በጣም ደስ የሚል ትዘምር ነበር።

pages.textAm: ንጉሡ ድምፁን ሰምቶ ወፏን ለመጋበዝ ፈለገ።
pages.textEn: The king heard the voice and wanted to invite the bird.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Nightingale, page 2: ንጉሡ ድምፁን ሰምቶ ወፏን ለመጋበዝ ፈለገ።

pages.textAm: በቤተመንግሥት ውስጥ ወርቃማ የሚዘምር መጫወቻም ነበር።
pages.textEn: Inside the palace, there was also a golden singing toy.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Nightingale, page 3: በቤተመንግሥት ውስጥ ወርቃማ የሚዘምር መጫወቻም ነበር።

pages.textAm: መጫወቻው ብሩህ ነበር፣ ግን የወፏ ዘፈን ልብን በሰላም ይሞላ ነበር።
pages.textEn: The toy was bright, but the bird’s song filled hearts with peace.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Nightingale, page 4: መጫወቻው ብሩህ ነበር፣ ግን የወፏ ዘፈን ልብን በሰላም ይሞላ ነበር።

pages.textAm: ንጉሡ ሁሉንም ቆም ብለው እንዲያዳምጡ ጠየቀ። “ይህ ሰላማዊ ዘፈን ከየት ይመጣል?” ብሎ ተገረመ።
pages.textEn: The king asked everyone to pause and listen, wondering where the peaceful song came from.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Nightingale, page 5: the king pausing in a palace garden to listen for a peaceful bird song.

pages.textAm: በዚያ ቀን ቤተመንግሥቱ በተፈጥሮ ዘፈን ተሞላ። አትክልተኛውም በፈገግታ “መንገዱን አሳይዎታለሁ” አለ።
pages.textEn: That day, the palace filled with natural song, and the gardener smiled and offered to show the way.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Nightingale, page 6: a palace garden filled with natural birdsong while the gardener offers to guide the king.

pages.textAm: አትክልተኛው ንጉሡን ወደ ጫካው ጫፍ መራው። “ወፏ በትንሽ ቅርንጫፍ ላይ ትዘምራለች፣ ግን ድምጿ ትልቅ ደስታ ይሰጣል” አለ።
pages.textEn: The gardener led the king to the garden edge where the little bird sang.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Gardener guiding a kind king toward a small nightingale in a garden tree.

pages.textAm: ንጉሡ የወፏን ዘፈን ሲሰማ በጸጥታ ተቀመጠ። የተፈጥሮ ድምፅ ልቡን እንደ ለስላሳ ነፋስ አረጋጋው።
pages.textEn: When the king heard the bird, the natural song calmed his heart.
pages.illustrationPrompt: King sitting quietly under a tree while a nightingale sings.

pages.textAm: ወርቃማው መጫወቻ በቤተመንግሥት ውስጥ ይብራ ነበር። ንጉሡ ግን “ብርሃን ደስ ይላል፣ ሕያው ዘፈን ግን ልብን ይነካል” አለ።
pages.textEn: The golden toy sparkled, but the king learned that living song touches the heart.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Golden singing toy on a palace table beside open windows to the garden.

pages.textAm: ንጉሡ ወፏን በወርቅ ቤት ለመዝጋት አልፈለገም። “በአትክልቱ ውስጥ በነፃ ዘምሪ፣ ስትፈልጊም ጎብኚን” ብሎ ጠየቃት።
pages.textEn: The king chose to let the nightingale sing freely in the garden.
pages.illustrationPrompt: King opening a palace window so a nightingale can fly freely.

pages.textAm: ወፏ በየጠዋቱ ትንሽ ዘፈን ትሰጥ ነበር። ንጉሡም ከአሁን በኋላ ቀላል ውበትን በምስጋና ማየት ተማረ።
pages.textEn: Each morning, the bird sang, and the king learned to be grateful for simple beauty.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Morning palace garden with a nightingale singing and the king smiling gratefully.
